title: Listening - Part 2
description: For each question, choose the correct answer.
instructions: You will hear six short extracts. For questions 8–13, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best according to what you hear.

extracts.context: You will hear two friends talking about a birthday meal.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: Have you chosen somewhere for your birthday meal? || Man: I wanted that Italian restaurant near the station, but they're fully booked. || Woman: What about the place by the river? || Man: I looked at it, but the menu is too formal. [8]Let's just go to the café we always use; everyone likes the food and nobody has to dress up[/8]. I'll book a big table. || Woman: Well, I hope it works out, because it sounds as if you've thought about it carefully.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Have you chosen somewhere for your birthday meal?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I wanted that Italian restaurant near the station, but they're fully booked.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: What about the place by the river?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I looked at it, but the menu is too formal. Let's just go to the café we always use; everyone likes the food and nobody has to dress up. I'll book a big table.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Well, I hope it works out, because it sounds as if you've thought about it carefully.

questions.question: Why does the man choose the café?

options.A: It will suit all his friends.
options.B: It is close to the station.

questions.explanation: He says everyone likes the food there and nobody has to dress up.

extracts.context: You will hear a woman talking about a new exercise app.
extracts.audio_script: Man: Are you still using that exercise app? || Woman: Yes. It sends reminders, which is useful, and the videos are clear. || Man: Has it helped you get fitter? || Woman: A little, but [9]what I like most is that I can choose ten-minute sessions when I'm busy[/9]. I don't always have time for a full class. || Man: Anyway, let me know what happens, because I'm interested to hear how it goes next time. || Woman: Yes, and at least you can plan better now, which should make things less stressful.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Are you still using that exercise app?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Yes. It sends reminders, which is useful, and the videos are clear.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Has it helped you get fitter?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: A little, but what I like most is that I can choose ten-minute sessions when I'm busy. I don't always have time for a full class.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Anyway, let me know what happens, because I'm interested to hear how it goes next time.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Yes, and at least you can plan better now, which should make things less stressful.

questions.question: What does the woman like most about the app?

options.A: It reminds her to exercise every day.

questions.explanation: She says she likes choosing ten-minute sessions when she is busy.

extracts.context: You will hear two students talking about a group project.
extracts.audio_script: Man: Have you heard from Liam about the project? || Woman: Not since Monday. He said he'd make the slides, but he hasn't sent anything. || Man: That's annoying. || Woman: It is. [10]The rest of us have finished our parts, but we can't practise the presentation until his slides arrive[/10]. I don't want to complain to the teacher yet. || Woman: Yes, and at least you can plan better now, which should make things less stressful. || Man: That's true. It's much better to notice the problem early than be surprised later.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Have you heard from Liam about the project?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Not since Monday. He said he'd make the slides, but he hasn't sent anything.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: That's annoying.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: It is. The rest of us have finished our parts, but we can't practise the presentation until his slides arrive. I don't want to complain to the teacher yet.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Yes, and at least you can plan better now, which should make things less stressful.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: That's true. It's much better to notice the problem early than be surprised later.

questions.question: What is the problem with the project?

options.A: The teacher has changed the deadline.
options.B: The group has not chosen a topic.
options.C: One student has not completed his task.

questions.explanation: The woman says Liam has not sent the slides, so one student has not completed his task.

extracts.context: You will hear a man telling a friend about a hotel breakfast.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: Was the hotel breakfast good? || Man: Better than I expected. I usually avoid hotel breakfasts because the coffee is weak and everything tastes the same. || Woman: So what was different? || Man: [11]They cooked eggs and pancakes when you asked, instead of leaving them under hot lights[/11]. The dining room was busy, but the food felt fresh. || Man: That's true. It's much better to notice the problem early than be surprised later. || Woman: Maybe you can tell me more about it tomorrow, when you've had time to think.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Was the hotel breakfast good?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Better than I expected. I usually avoid hotel breakfasts because the coffee is weak and everything tastes the same.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: So what was different?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: They cooked eggs and pancakes when you asked, instead of leaving them under hot lights. The dining room was busy, but the food felt fresh.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: That's true. It's much better to notice the problem early than be surprised later.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Maybe you can tell me more about it tomorrow, when you've had time to think.

questions.question: What did the man like about the breakfast?

options.A: Some food was prepared specially for guests.
options.B: The dining room was quiet and comfortable.
options.C: The coffee tasted better than usual.

questions.explanation: He says they cooked eggs and pancakes when guests asked.

extracts.context: You will hear two friends talking about a computer game.
extracts.audio_script: Man: Have you tried the new racing game yet? || Woman: Yes, my brother bought it. It looks fantastic, and the cars are easy to control. || Man: So you recommend it? || Woman: For some people, yes, but [12]there are only a few tracks, so after one evening I felt I'd seen nearly everything[/12]. I expected more variety. || Woman: Maybe you can tell me more about it tomorrow, when you've had time to think. || Man: Good point. I suppose it's one of those things you only understand after trying it yourself.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Have you tried the new racing game yet?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Yes, my brother bought it. It looks fantastic, and the cars are easy to control.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: So you recommend it?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: For some people, yes, but there are only a few tracks, so after one evening I felt I'd seen nearly everything. I expected more variety.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Maybe you can tell me more about it tomorrow, when you've had time to think.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Good point. I suppose it's one of those things you only understand after trying it yourself.

questions.question: What does the woman say about the game?

options.A: The cars are hard to control.

questions.explanation: She says there are only a few tracks and she expected more variety.

extracts.context: You will hear a woman talking about a local market.
extracts.audio_script: Speaker: I went to the new Sunday market expecting to buy vegetables, but there weren't many food stalls. There were lots of handmade things, though, and I found a birthday present for my sister. [13]The only thing that annoyed me was the music from the stage, which was so loud that it was hard to talk to the stallholders[/13]. I'll go again, but earlier next time. || Speaker: I told a friend about it later, and she said she had experienced something very similar.

dialogue.speaker: speaker
dialogue.text: I went to the new Sunday market expecting to buy vegetables, but there weren't many food stalls. There were lots of handmade things, though, and I found a birthday present for my sister. The only thing that annoyed me was the music from the stage, which was so loud that it was hard to talk to the stallholders. I'll go again, but earlier next time.

dialogue.speaker: speaker
dialogue.text: I told a friend about it later, and she said she had experienced something very similar.

questions.question: What annoyed the woman at the market?

options.A: There were too few handmade gifts.

questions.explanation: She says the music was so loud it was hard to talk to the stallholders.
